summary.text: Fundamental and historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP.

overview.description: Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive; and Energy Generation and Storage. The company offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty maintenance services and collision, automotive insurance services, as well as part sales and retail merchandise sale. It also provides sedans and sport utility vehicles through direct and ...
overview.financialIntro: In 2025, Tesla's revenue was $94.83 billion, a decrease of -2.93% compared to the previous year's $97.69 billion. Earnings were $3.79 billion, a decrease of -46.79%.
overview.analystIntro: According to 45 analysts, the average rating for TSLA stock is "Buy." The 12-month stock price target is $413.57, which is an increase of 29.48% from the latest price.
